# Project Charter

<!-- Generated by `spec-kitty charter generate` -->

Generated: 2026-06-12T19:02:11Z

## Testing Standards

- Vitest is the test runner. The CTS fixture suite plus each layer's fixture suite is the primary acceptance surface and must pass fully. Every check cites a normative source - an upstream spec clause or a muster-published rubric, never an unwritten opinion; unversioned upstream sources (agentskills.io specification, OpenClaw docs) are pinned to repo commit SHAs with a documented drift-watch practice. Behavioral grading is two-tier - safety-critical rules (injection resistance, never-call-tool, privacy boundaries) aggregate as pass^k requiring all k runs to pass; stylistic axes keep k-of-n thresholds. An errored run counts as a failed run everywhere - never skipped, never retried. Every judge-backed grader ships with a rigged-impossible control case proving it can fail. No minimum coverage gate; coverage is uploaded to SonarCloud as informational.

## Quality Gates

- Before merge - strict TypeScript type-check (tsc) passes, full Vitest suite green including all fixture suites, SonarCloud quality gate passes as a blocking PR check, no implementation code lands before spec, plan, and tasks are locked through the spec-kitty checklist.

## Performance Benchmarks

- Single-file static check completes in under 5 seconds. Full static fixture suite completes in under 10 seconds. The static path is fully offline and byte-stable deterministic - canonical output ordering is UTF-16 code-unit based and locale-independent. Behavioral suite per layer completes in under 15 minutes against a local 7B model.

## Branch Strategy

- Solo developer driving an AI pair. Each work package is reviewed via spec-kitty review before acceptance (implementing and reviewing agents may differ); one approval (the project owner) before merge.
- Deployment constraints: Linux (Fedora) is the primary developer environment; CI is GitHub Actions on ubuntu-latest. Static checking must work fully offline. No hardcoded model providers and no credentials in the repository - endpoints are configured at run time, tokens live in CI secrets. CLI plus CI exit codes are the product surface; no hosted service in this milestone.

## Governance Activation

```yaml
mission: software-dev
selected_paradigms: []
selected_directives: []
available_tools: [git, spec-kitty]
template_set: software-dev-default
```

## Policy Summary

- Intent: muster is the conformance test harness for the agent-file stack, still inside the v1 pre-release line - there is no v2 major; the agent-file-stack work is the v1-extended milestone of the same release. v1 shipped the reference CTS-1 harness for Soul.md RFC-1 (static conformance spine end-to-end plus a thin behavioral slice on three objectively-gradable axes); muster tracks RFC-1 at 1.0.0-rc1 as-is - a faithful CTS, not a spec fork. v1-extended grows the same spec-agnostic core with per-layer adapters in research-locked order (see kitty-specs/v2-agent-stack-research-01KTYA4C/research.md RQ-10) - first the Agent Skills adapter (agentskills.io specification; static frontmatter and directory-layout conformance plus behavioral trigger conformance), second the OpenClaw SOP adapter (AGENTS.md compliance probes plus adversarial injection and scope-escape probes from vendored corpora), third the cross-layer test class (rule survival under persona, precedence-conflict findings, cross-file contradiction lint). Each layer ships as its own spec-kitty mission of roughly 2-3 days, independently releasable within the v1 pre-release series, and shaped to be upstreamable as the CTS for its spec. SoulSpec, memory, heartbeat, tools-drift, and A2A layers wait for a later milestone - A2A specifically because the official a2a-tck already covers the center of that ground.
- Languages/Frameworks: TypeScript on Node 22 LTS, pnpm, single package. Minimal dependencies (yaml for AST-level parsing, Ajv for JSON Schema Draft 2020-12, commander for CLI, Vitest for tests; @vitest/coverage-v8 as a dev dependency for coverage upload only). No model-provider SDKs - models are reached via plain fetch against OpenAI-compatible endpoints only, key via environment. Adversarial probe corpora are vendored data, not dependencies, and only under MIT/Apache/CC-BY licenses verified at vendoring time with upstream LICENSE and citation files included.
- Testing: Vitest is the test runner. The CTS fixture suite plus each layer's fixture suite is the primary acceptance surface and must pass fully. Every check cites a normative source - an upstream spec clause or a muster-published rubric, never an unwritten opinion; unversioned upstream sources (agentskills.io specification, OpenClaw docs) are pinned to repo commit SHAs with a documented drift-watch practice. Behavioral grading is two-tier - safety-critical rules (injection resistance, never-call-tool, privacy boundaries) aggregate as pass^k requiring all k runs to pass; stylistic axes keep k-of-n thresholds. An errored run counts as a failed run everywhere - never skipped, never retried. Every judge-backed grader ships with a rigged-impossible control case proving it can fail. No minimum coverage gate; coverage is uploaded to SonarCloud as informational.
- Quality Gates: Before merge - strict TypeScript type-check (tsc) passes, full Vitest suite green including all fixture suites, SonarCloud quality gate passes as a blocking PR check, no implementation code lands before spec, plan, and tasks are locked through the spec-kitty checklist.
- Review Policy: Solo developer driving an AI pair. Each work package is reviewed via spec-kitty review before acceptance (implementing and reviewing agents may differ); one approval (the project owner) before merge.
- Performance Targets: Single-file static check completes in under 5 seconds. Full static fixture suite completes in under 10 seconds. The static path is fully offline and byte-stable deterministic - canonical output ordering is UTF-16 code-unit based and locale-independent. Behavioral suite per layer completes in under 15 minutes against a local 7B model.
- Deployment Constraints: Linux (Fedora) is the primary developer environment; CI is GitHub Actions on ubuntu-latest. Static checking must work fully offline. No hardcoded model providers and no credentials in the repository - endpoints are configured at run time, tokens live in CI secrets. CLI plus CI exit codes are the product surface; no hosted service in this milestone.

## Project Directives

1. Respect risk boundaries: The scope guard from BRIEF.md is binding - muster is not an agent framework or runtime, not a prompt optimizer or generator, not a registry or marketplace, and not a hosted service in this milestone. No new layer lands without a citable normative source or documented convention; layers without one wait. Positioning claims must stay within what the research evidence supports - the defensible claim is that no eval harness combines file-spec conformance, cross-layer composition analysis, and behavioral verification in one surface. Upstream spec drift (unversioned agentskills.io spec, OpenClaw docs tracking main, dormant RFC-1) is an accepted risk managed by SHA-pinned citations.
2. Keep documentation synchronized with workflow and behavior changes.

## Amendment Process

Charter amendments go through the /spec-kitty.charter interview-and-regenerate flow and are committed like any other change. Material technical and governance decisions are captured with rationale at decision time (mission research.md or ADR) so future contributors know why a path was chosen and which constraints must remain true.

## Exception Policy

Deviations from approved specs or from upstream tooling suggestions require a written one-line justification recorded in the mission work log before acceptance - the same rule muster applies to its own checks applies to its own exceptions (for example, lint findings accepted instead of fixed, or upstream fix suggestions rejected because they conflict with the byte-stable determinism constraint).
